name: USS Ronald Reagan CSG — Philippine Sea Repositioning
detail: US 7th Fleet repositioned carrier strike group led by USS Ronald Reagan (CVN-76) from South China Sea to Philippine Sea east of Taiwan during Aug 2022 PLA exercises. Deliberate deterrent positioning without direct strait transit to signal US presence without escalation. Additional destroyer and cruiser assets contributed to the show of force.

name: Japan Formal Protest — PLA Missiles in Japan EEZ
detail: Japan's Ministry of Defense identified 5 PLA ballistic missiles landing in Japan's Exclusive Economic Zone SW of Okinawa on August 4–5, 2022. PM Fumio Kishida filed formal diplomatic protest calling it 'a serious problem affecting Japan's national security.' The incident galvanized domestic Japanese support for the eventual doubling of Japan's defense budget announced in December 2022.

name: Taiwan ROCAF Sustained Air Scramble Operations
detail: Taiwan's ROCAF activates sustained air defense scramble operations throughout PLA exercises, August 2022 onward. Fighters intercept and shadow PLA aircraft crossing ADIZ. Taiwan MND publishes real-time ADIZ incursion notifications for public transparency. Sustained scramble pace causes operational stress — rising sortie rates correlated with training accident uptick.

name: US NDAA FY2023 — $10 Billion Taiwan Defense Financing
detail: National Defense Authorization Act for FY2023 (signed December 23, 2022) authorized up to $10 billion in Foreign Military Financing for Taiwan over five years, streamlined arms sale processes, and codified expanded US-Taiwan military training. The law represents the most substantial legislative Taiwan defense commitment since the 1979 Taiwan Relations Act.

name: Regular US Navy Taiwan Strait Transits
detail: US Navy maintains 1-2 Taiwan Strait transits per month using destroyers, cruisers, and occasionally aircraft carriers. Following August 2022 crisis, transits became more publicly announced and symbolically significant. PLA navy ships shadow all transits at close range. G7 allies (Canada, UK, France, Australia) have also conducted independent Taiwan Strait transits to demonstrate multilateral freedom of navigation support.

name: Taiwan Extends Mandatory Military Service to 1 Year
detail: Taiwan President Tsai Ing-wen announced in December 2022 that mandatory military service would return to a full year for men born in 2005 or later (beginning January 2024), reversing a 2013 reduction to 4 months. The change signals Taiwan's recognition of inadequate reserve force readiness. Enhanced training curriculum includes combined arms operations, asymmetric warfare, and urban combat scenarios.

name: Trump Administration Approves Record $11.15B Arms Sale
detail: December 18, 2025: US DSCA notifies Congress of the largest-ever arms sale to Taiwan ($11.154 billion). Package includes HIMARS rocket systems, M109A6 Paladin howitzers, drones, anti-armor munitions, and supporting equipment. Despite Trump's rhetorical ambiguity about Taiwan defense, the sale demonstrates continued institutional US support. Beijing calls it 'a grave violation of China's sovereignty.'

name: Taiwan Accelerates Asymmetric 'Porcupine Strategy'
detail: Taiwan's MND accelerates asymmetric defense procurement throughout 2022-2026: Harpoon Coastal Defense System (100 launchers, 400 missiles); Hsiung Feng III anti-ship missiles; mobile HIMARS; drone swarm acquisition programs; indigenous submarine program (first submarine ROCS Hai Kun launched Sep 2023). Asymmetric strategy aims to raise PLA invasion costs beyond acceptable levels without matching PLA conventionally.

name: Japan Doubles Defense Budget to 2% GDP by 2027
detail: Japan's government approved a 5-year $315 billion defense buildup plan in December 2022, doubling defense spending from 1% to 2% of GDP by 2027. Package includes Tomahawk cruise missiles (strike capability against mainland targets), F-35B short-takeoff jets, Aegis Ashore missile defense, and significant ISE (island security) enhancements in Ryukyu island chain — directly adjacent to Taiwan. Explicitly motivated by Taiwan Strait security concerns.

name: US Gains Access to 4 Additional Philippines Bases (EDCA)
detail: February 2023: Philippines grants US access to 4 additional military bases under Enhanced Defense Cooperation Agreement (EDCA), bringing total to 9 bases. Key sites include bases in northern Luzon province (Cagayan, Isabela) directly facing Taiwan Strait's southern approach. Access significantly enhances US operational flexibility for Taiwan contingency — reduces reliance on Guam and Okinawa, adds southern approach vectors.
